rules.name: metric_catalog_and_thresholds
rules.overview: Reference for the full 28-metric catalog, threshold mechanics, and the 5-layer override model. Read this when the partner is at Phase 2 (design) or Phase 4 (tuning).

category.name: safety
category.description: Detect harmful or adversarial content. Score is the model's confidence that the content IS risky — higher = more risky.

category.name: rag_generation
category.description: Score the LLM's generated answer against the user query and retrieved context. Low score = risky (hallucination or off-topic).

category.name: rag_retrieval
category.description: Score the retriever's output. Built on Context Relevance scores per document, then aggregated via classical IR formulas.
category.critical: context MUST be a list[str], one entry per retrieved document. Single-string context gives degenerate scores.

category.name: quality
category.description: Output quality metrics. 2 LLM-judge (require WXG_PROJECT_ID), 3 deterministic.

category.name: pattern
category.description: Pure-Python pattern matching. Score is 1.0 on match, 0.0 on no match. No flag state (binary).

category.name: tool_call
category.note: Tool Call Accuracy returns None when there are no syntactic issues (the SDK doesn't emit a score for valid calls). This is treated as Pass by the threshold logic.

layer.name: constructor_argument
layer.use_case: App-level policy baked into evaluator at startup.

layer.name: yaml_config_file
layer.yaml_shape: defaults: safety: 0.6 rag: 0.15 quality: 0.1 metrics: "PII Detection": 0.7 "Faithfulness": 0.15
layer.use_case: Per-deployment policy, version-controlled separately from code.

layer.name: environment_variables
layer.use_case: Per-environment (dev/staging/prod) policy. Set in container env or CI.

layer.name: package_defaults
layer.table: Safety: block=0.65 flag=0.4 | RAG: block=0.1 flag=0.3 | Pattern: block=0.5 (no flag) | Quality: block=0.1 flag=0.3

flag_block_invariants.high_is_risk: flag_value must be strictly LESS THAN block value. Example: flag=0.4, block=0.65.
flag_block_invariants.low_is_risk: flag_value must be strictly GREATER THAN block value. Example: block=0.1, flag=0.3.
flag_block_invariants.auto_drop: When `with_value()` is called with a new block that invalidates the existing flag, the flag is auto-dropped (set to None). To keep a Flag state at the new block level, also pass flag_thresholds={metric: new_flag}.

mode.name: explicit_metrics
mode.semantics: Strict — every named metric runs; missing required fields raise MissingFieldError.
mode.use_when: Production: tight, deliberate metric set per choke point.

mode.name: categories
mode.semantics: Lenient — runs every metric in those categories whose required_fields are satisfied; silently skips others.
mode.use_when: Exploratory: "give me everything safety-related I can run with the data I have."

mode.name: auto_select
mode.semantics: Runs every metric in the registry whose required_fields are satisfied.
mode.use_when: Quick sanity check; not recommended for production (unpredictable cost as catalog grows).

field_availability_matrix.overview:
Most partners don't have ALL 7 input fields. Bob's job is to tell them up-front exactly which metrics will run given their data shape, so they don't assume they're getting all 28 when they're actually getting a subset.
This matrix maps common partner data shapes to the metric subset that will run. Use it during Phase 2 (Design) to set expectations before any code is written.
field_availability_matrix.key: WXG_PROJECT_ID env var status also matters: without it, ALL THREE LLM-judge metrics (Answer Completeness, Conciseness, Tool Call Relevance) are omitted from the registry regardless of which fields the partner supplies. Counts below assume WXG_PROJECT_ID IS set.

shape.name: input-text-only
shape.runs: 10 generic safety metrics (HAP, PII, Harm, SocialBias, Jailbreak, Violence, Profanity, Unethical, SexualContent, Evasiveness) + HarmEngagement
shape.skipped: PromptSafetyRisk (needs system_prompt), TopicRelevance (system_prompt), Keyword/RegexDetection (params), all RAG-generation (need generated_text + context), all RAG-retrieval (need context), all Quality (need generated_text), all Tool-call (need tool_calls)

shape.name: input-text-and-system-prompt
shape.typical_for: System-prompt-aware input safety (catch off-topic queries against a known system prompt; detect prompt-injection).

shape.name: input-and-output
shape.runs: 10 generic safety metrics (will run on either input or output side), + HarmEngagement (input-only) + Answer Completeness (input+output) + Conciseness, TextGradeLevel, TextReadingEase, UnsuccessfulRequests (output-only) — 10 + 1 + 1 + 4 = 16. Wait: of the 10 generics, they accept_fields=either, so they run once on whichever was supplied — pick the one you want via explicit metric names; auto-select runs them on input.
shape.typical_for: Non-RAG agent (single-shot LLM call without retrieval).

shape.name: input-output-context-string
shape.runs: Above (input+output) + RAG generation (Answer Relevance, Context Relevance, Faithfulness)
shape.skipped: RAG retrieval (needs context as list[str], not single str)
shape.typical_for: RAG agent where the partner only cares about generation quality, not retrieval quality.

shape.name: input-and-context-list
shape.runs: 11 input-eligible safety + RAG retrieval (Retrieval Precision, Hit Rate, Reciprocal Rank)
shape.typical_for: Pre-LLM retrieval guardrail. Run AFTER retrieve() but BEFORE the LLM call so partner can skip the LLM if HitRate=0.

shape.name: full-rag-with-context-string
shape.typical_for: Most full-featured chat-style RAG agent with a defined persona.

shape.name: tool-using-agent
shape.runs: 11 input-eligible safety + Tool Call Accuracy + Tool Call Relevance
shape.typical_for: Agent that uses function calling; checks the LLM picked the right tool with valid args.

shape.name: compliance-blocklist
shape.typical_for: Partners who must enforce custom keyword blocklists (competitor names, internal project codenames, regulated terms).

how_to_inspect_at_runtime.code: # Show partner exactly which metrics will fire for their data shape: ev = GuardrailsEvaluator() sample = {"input_text": "...", "generated_text": "...", "context": "..."} bundle = ev.evaluate(**sample) print("Will run:", bundle.metrics_evaluated) # Or list all + check required_fields against the sample: for entry in ev.registry: satisfied = entry.required_fields.issubset(sample.keys()) print(f"{entry.name}: {'will run' if satisfied else 'SKIPPED — needs ' + str(entry.required_fields)}")

field_availability_matrix.bob_workflow_implication: Phase 2 (Design integration) MUST include a "given your data, here's what runs" enumeration. Don't let the partner discover at deployment time that 6 metrics they assumed were running aren't. See RULE 18.

per_partner_policy_pattern.description: Different partners (or tenants) may need different threshold policies.

approach_1.name: yaml_per_partner
approach_1.how: Keep one YAML per partner: `thresholds.partner-acme.yaml`, `thresholds.partner-globex.yaml`. Switch via GUARDRAILS_CONFIG_PATH env var at deploy time.

approach_2.name: per_call_at_runtime
approach_2.how: Look up the partner's policy in your database; pass thresholds= per request.
approach_2.example: partner_policy = db.get_policy(partner_id) bundle = ev.evaluate( input_text=query, thresholds=partner_policy["block"], flag_thresholds=partner_policy["flag"], fallback_messages=partner_policy["fallback"], )

approach_3.name: multiple_evaluators
approach_3.how: Build N GuardrailsEvaluator instances (one per partner) at startup with different threshold_overrides=. Look up the right one per request.
